Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Crazy Tatjana STAT that’ll blow your mind

There are some startling statistics to back up Tatjana Smith’s case as South Africa’s greatest-ever Olympian, but this particular stat is so extraordinary, it’ll blow your mind.

Tatjana Smith has called time on her illustrious swimming career, announcing her retirement soon after she created history by becoming South Africa’s greatest-achieving Olympian, at the 2024 Paris Games.

Her silver medal performance in the 200m breaststroke at the Olympics in Thursday night, ensure that she finishes the games with two medals, having clinched gold in the 100m breaststroke earlier this week.

This equalled her medals tally at the 2020 Olympics, where she also won one gold (200m breaststroke) and one silver (100m breaststroke).

And this is where the remarkable statistic comes in: As it stands,Team SAhas won sevens medals at the last two editions of the Olympic Games.

And with four of them coming from Tatjana Smith, she has won57% of all SA’s Olympicmedals in Tokyo 2020 and Paris 2024.

Additionally, coupled with her four Olympic medals, she bows out with four medals (three gold and a silver) at the Commonwealth Games and three World Championship medals (one gold and two silver), as well as one world record.

According to theTeam SA website, Tatjana is a  staunch Christian, who has always believed there’s a bigger plan and goal to her life than winning swimming medals.

“I’m looking forward to my life outside of swimming.” she said in making her announcement.

“It was an amazing battle and I’ve never raced Kate (Douglass) in an Olympic final before. I couldn’t have asked for a better way to end my career in a very tough battle. I’ve always loved competing.”

In confirming her retirement, Tatjana Smith said: “Achievements fall away. You are remembered for who you are and I want to be remembered for bringing people joy. Swimming is a part of a season in your life and now I’m excited to live life.”
